site.title: Carnelian Docs
site.tagline: Documentation for the local-first AI workspace.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.summary: Install Carnelian and run your first local chat.

sections.text: Carnelian is a cross-platform desktop app that runs AI **locally** through [Ollama](https://ollama.com), and optionally through cloud providers you configure. This page gets you from zero to your first reply.
1. Install Ollama
Carnelian needs a local Ollama server with at least one model pulled. You can install Ollama in one click from inside the app (Settings → Models → Local AI), or set it up yourself:

ollama serve
ollama pull llama3.1
The Project agent needs a tool-calling-capable model, e.g. `llama3.1`, `qwen2.5`, or `mistral-nemo`.
2. Get Carnelian
Download a self-contained build for Windows or Linux from the [releases page](https://github.com/Sagiv440/Carnelian_AI/releases), or build from source:

git clone https://github.com/Sagiv440/Carnelian_AI
dotnet run --project src/AI_Interface
3. Pick a model and chat
TODO: walk through the model picker, the connection indicator, and sending a first message.

pages.title: The Four Modes
pages.summary: Chat, Web Search, Deep Research, and Project.

sections.text: Carnelian has four operating modes, chosen from the sidebar and composer rather than a single picker. Every mode is provider-agnostic — pick any model and it just works.

pages.title: Agents & Personas
pages.summary: Selectable personas with skills and tool allow-lists.

sections.text: An agent is a selectable persona whose voice is layered into every mode's system prompt. Carnelian ships five built-ins and lets you author your own, globally or per-project.

items: **Lead** — the orchestrator that delegates subtasks to specialists in Project mode.

pages.title: Project Agent
pages.summary: A tool-using agent that builds inside your projects.

sections.text: Point the Project agent at a folder and give it a goal. It plans, reads, edits files, runs commands, and (when allowed) installs — all confined to the project directory, under approval rules you set.
